creator: michael.adm@gmail.com
text:
Based on your question, I installed clean five distributions of different release times on another hypervisor, with the same parameters and equipment.
1. FreeBSD-13.0-CURRENT-amd64-20200130-r357276-disc1.iso
2. FreeBSD-14.0-CURRENT-amd64-20210422-df456a1fcf7-246266-disc1.iso
3. FreeBSD-14.0-CURRENT-amd64-20220203-e2fe58d61b7-252875-disc1.iso
4. FreeBSD-14.2-STABLE-amd64-20241212-4db4ed368037-269898-disc1.iso
5. FreeBSD-15.0-CURRENT-amd64-20241207-0fd7b4655666-274067-disc1.iso

Yes, the behavior with drivers version 23.x.xxx and 24.x.xxx is the same as on the first hypervisor.

With 23.x.xxx versions of the driver everything works as it should.

With 24.x.xxx versions of the driver, vlans do not work in VMs with guest FriBSD OS in all five installed versions out of the box.
